title: 〜ようになる・〜ようにする
description: 〜ようになる marks a change — coming to a point where something is possible or habitual ("came to be able to"). 〜ようにする is about deliberate effort — making sure something happens ("try to / make a point of"). A bare verb ＋ ように also means "so that."

content.text: Dictionary or potential form plus ようになる describes reaching a new state — often a new ability or a habit that developed over time. It's the go-to way to say "came to be able to" or "started to (regularly)."

content.japanese: 毎日（まいにち）練習（れんしゅう）して、ようやく弾（ひ）けるようになった。
content.english: After practicing every day, I finally became able to play it.

content.japanese: 日本語（にほんご）が話（はな）せるようになりたい。
content.english: I want to become able to speak Japanese.

content.japanese: トイレトレーニングのあと、一緒（いっしょ）に寝（ね）るようになりました。
content.english: After potty training, they came to sleep together with us.

content.text: Dictionary or negative form plus ようにする means you make a conscious effort toward something. In its 〜ようにしている form, it describes an ongoing habit you keep up on purpose.

content.japanese: 毎日（まいにち）野菜（やさい）を食（た）べるようにしている。
content.english: I make a point of eating vegetables every day.

content.japanese: 朝（あさ）9時（じ）か10時（じ）からのクラスを受（う）けるようにしています。
content.english: I try to take classes that start at 9 or 10 in the morning.

content.text: A verb (often potential or negative) plus ように on its own means "so that / in order to" — it states the purpose of the following action. This usually uses a verb the subject can't fully control, like a potential form.

content.japanese: 映画（えいが）が分（わ）かるように、アニメを見（み）なきゃいけないですか。
content.english: Do I have to watch anime so that I can understand movies?

content.japanese: 後（うし）ろの人（ひと）にも聞（き）こえるように、大（おお）きな声（こえ）で話（はな）した。
content.english: I spoke in a loud voice so the people in back could hear too.

examples.japanese: 毎日（まいにち）練習（れんしゅう）して、ようやく弾（ひ）けるようになった。
examples.english: After practicing every day, I finally became able to play it.

examples.japanese: 日本語（にほんご）が話（はな）せるようになりたい。
examples.english: I want to become able to speak Japanese.

examples.japanese: トイレトレーニングのあと、一緒（いっしょ）に寝（ね）るようになりました。
examples.english: After potty training, they came to sleep together with us.

examples.japanese: 毎日（まいにち）野菜（やさい）を食（た）べるようにしている。
examples.english: I make a point of eating vegetables every day.

examples.japanese: 朝（あさ）9時（じ）か10時（じ）からのクラスを受（う）けるようにしています。
examples.english: I try to take classes that start at 9 or 10 in the morning.

examples.japanese: 映画（えいが）が分（わ）かるように、アニメを見（み）なきゃいけないですか。
examples.english: Do I have to watch anime so that I can understand movies?

examples.japanese: 後（うし）ろの人（ひと）にも聞（き）こえるように、大（おお）きな声（こえ）で話（はな）した。
examples.english: I spoke in a loud voice so the people in back could hear too.
